etymology.description: 戌(戌) (xū, "halberd") + 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") = a woman exerting force with a halberd -> might
etymology.interpretation: The character 威 (wēi, "might") combines 戌(戌) (xū, "halberd") and 女(女) (nǚ, "woman") to depict the image of a woman wielding a halberd, a weapon of authority and power. This image metaphorically conveys the concept of strength and might, suggesting a commanding presence.

examples.chinese: 狮子散发出威严的气息(獅子散發出威嚴的氣息)。
examples.pinyin: Shīzi sànfā chū wēiyán de qìxī.
examples.english: The lion exudes an aura of might.

examples.chinese: 那位将军的威力令人畏惧(那位將軍的威力令人畏懼)。
examples.pinyin: Nà wèi jiāngjūn de wēilì lìng rén wèijù.
examples.english: The power of that general is intimidating.

examples.chinese: 城堡在山顶上展现出雄伟(城堡在山頂上展現出雄偉)，仿佛在宣扬它的威力(彷佛在宣揚它的威力)。
examples.pinyin: Chéngbǎo zài shāndǐng shàng zhǎnxiàn chū xióngwěi, fǎngfú zài xuānyáng tā de wēilì.
examples.english: The castle stands majestically on the hilltop as if proclaiming its might.
